_comment: Codex runs command hooks with the session cwd as the working directory, NOT a guaranteed project root (Codex may be started from a subdirectory), and provides no project-dir env var for non-plugin hooks (verified against developers.openai.com/codex/hooks, 2026-06-23). Bare relative paths like 'ai/codex/hook-scripts/...' therefore break when the session cwd is a subdir. Per the Codex docs' own recommendation we resolve the script path from the git root via 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel'. This mirrors the $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR absolute-path rule for Claude Code hooks (see docs/design/workflow-model/04-harness-acceptance-criteria.md Part B §9).
